_description: Sample: Subject Revision Strategy — Biology, NEET 2025, Academic Planner persona

strategy.subject: Biology
should_revise_chapters: Structural Organisation in Animals and Plants
top_5_micro_topics: Mendelian Genetics — Law of Independent Assortment

high_risk_clusters.subject: Biology
high_risk_clusters.strategic_note: Questions from this cluster frequently appear together in a single NEET paper — mastering Mendelian genetics anchors 4–6 marks

strategy.executive_summary: Biology commands ~50% of the NEET paper (90 of 180 marks). For 2025, the PRAJNA prediction engine signals a rising trend in Genetics and a stable, high-weight signal for Human Physiology. Tier-A allocation: Genetics + Human Physiology = 60% of Biology study time. The Genetics Core cluster (Mendelian → Chromosomal Theory → Molecular Basis) has an 78% historical co-occurrence rate — treat them as a unit.
strategy.strategy_narrative:
ALLOCATION RECOMMENDATION (Academic Planner View):

Tier A — Critical (must cover in full):
• Genetics and Evolution [cluster importance: 0.91] — 40h
• Human Physiology [importance: 0.85] — 35h
• Plant Physiology [importance: 0.79] — 25h

Tier B — High ROI:
• Cell Structure and Function [importance: 0.75] — 20h
• Reproduction [importance: 0.72] — 20h
• Ecology and Environment [importance: 0.68] — 15h

Tier C — Optional / Low-signal:
• The Living World, Animal Kingdom — 10h combined

Risk flags:
• Biotechnology is a rising trend (+0.14 YoY signal) — consider upgrading from Tier B to Tier A for risk-averse students
• Ecology questions increased from 8% to 11% in last 2 years — do not neglect
• Confidence for Human Physiology cluster is 0.88 — highest in the subject

Estimated marks at risk if Tier A is skipped: 38–42 marks
